---
title: "About & Contact"
---

## Who Are We

We are Regina Ring and Yehuda Dinaii. Our journey into peroxisome biology began with a 
personal discovery: our daughters' sensorineural hearing loss is caused by the missense 
mutation **PEX26-F51L**. This finding set us on a path to understand the molecular 
mechanisms behind this mutation and its impact on peroxisome biogenesis.

---

## Regina Ring

Regina holds an MSc in Computer Science from [Tel Aviv University](https://english.tau.ac.il) 
and a BSc in Information Systems from the 
[Technion — Israel Institute of Technology](https://www.technion.ac.il/en/). She works as 
a Staff Software Engineer and contributes to the computational and infrastructural aspects 
of this research.

📧 [regina.ring@gmail.com](mailto:regina.ring@gmail.com)

---

## Yehuda Dinaii

Yehuda holds a PhD in Physics from the Weizmann Institute of Science, where his research 
focused on theoretical condensed matter physics. In recent years his research direction 
has shifted toward structural and computational biology, motivated by the search for a 
deeper understanding of the PEX26-F51L mutation and its consequences.

He is an Adjunct Lecturer at [Afeka Academic College of Engineering](https://www.afeka.ac.il/en/) 
and at [Achva Academic College](https://www.achva.ac.il/en/), and his current research 
focuses on structural and computational modeling of the PEX1/PEX6/PEX26 AAA-ATPase complex.

### Research Interests

- Structural and computational modeling of the PEX1/PEX6/PEX26 complex
- Conformational dynamics of PEX26 and the functional impact of disease-associated variants
- Peroxisome biogenesis and its connection to sensorineural hearing loss

---

📧 [yehuda.dinaii@gmail.com](mailto:yehuda.dinaii@gmail.com)
